title: 1. Installation
text: stable version package available CRAN servers, means can install regular install.packages() function. reason, installlation CRAN fails, might need look alternative methods.
code: install.packages("icosa")

title: 1. Installation
text: sake completeness simplicity, sources stable version also available GitHub. install.packages() function can used install source - install dependencies package, compiler installed system!
code: install.packages(     "https://zenodo.org/records/16992403/files/icosa_0.12.0.tar.gz?download=1",      repos=NULL, type="source")

title: 1. Installation
text: encounter technical problems package described anywhere, might want take look development version. Note still need compiler installed!

title: 1. Installation
text: simplest way install -development version via devtools package. just indicate want install -development devel branch.
code: devtools::install_github("icosa-grid/R-icosa@devel")

title: 1. Installation
text: can also install package manually following steps: Clone repository local hard drive. Make sure package dependencies, compiler installed: Windows, RTools include tools need, Mac, need XCode - use GNU/Linux, go deeper rabbit hole :). Open terminal navigate directory cloned. icosa directory visible . Run line terminal see error suggesting R found, add PATH environmental variable. R packages icosa depend installed, install manually, get error.
code: R CMD INSTALL icosa

title: Specifications

Although many metrics defined available simple application scenarios, can extended almost trivially combinations input data, may better uses cases. section reviews types input data used basis geographic shape analysis (especially taxon distribution data), later used systematically review develop specific methods.

title: Specifications
text: functions metrics S4 methods implemented multiple primary data input classes. primary input (following R tradition) parametrized x. x parameter can following can following input data structures: coordmat: two-column tabular structure inherits matrix. simplest input format, implies long-lat two columns. coorddat: data.frame geographic coordinates. column coordinates need specified, default long="long" lat="lat". sfpoints: multipoint object, either classes sf sfc. sfpolygons: multipolygon class object sf sfc. icell: Vector cell identifiers icosahedral grid (icosa::hexagrid icosa::trigrid), character vector. Note can used together igrid type icosahedral grid. rast: SpatRaster class object defined terra package. case input types, specific components can specified following parameters. - loc: location label coorddat - long: Column name indicating longitudes (character) - lat: Column name indicating latitudes (character) - tax: Column name indicating taxa, functional units can used split dataset.

title: Specifications
text: list additional parameters may necessary metrics. sf: ‘simple feature’ type object, usually polygon multipolygon style object class sf sfc p: Geographic coordinates (long, lat) single point given two-column matrix. icosa: icosahedral grid object defined either icosa::trigrid derived class icosa::hexagrid. mask: Another spatial structure allows exclusion certain area calculation metric. q: single real number range [0,1], expressing confidence critical interval, numeric. dm: distance matrix may replace default distance matrix (e.g. great circle distance matrix). Frequently used refine spatial environment, e.g. consider distances water, land, etc. unique: logical switch indicating whether duplicate coordinates omitted calculations, can effects results cases (e.g. centroid calculation). sake efficiency transparency, set TRUE default, means every coordinate pair considered calculations. effects can explored deliberate inclusion duplicates.

title: Specifications
text: list parameters change behavior functions. - full: Boolean switch indicating whether single estimate diagnostic/partial data returned object. - plot: Boolean switch indicating whether standard visualization method executed . Defaults FALSE. - iter: Natural number indicating number iterations executed. Usually used Monte-Carlo estimation-based metrics.

title: Specifications
text: default behavior estimators return single numeric value every distribution, result argumentation full=FALSE. can switched TRUE, make range shape metrics output objects inherit orange class. Every orange class object must least two elements: - $estimate: numeric estimate calculated function. - $plot: expression can used plot object - partials: Additional list elements can used inspect accuracy method, well plotting additional ftod

Records randomly drawn q proportion overall cover reached.

title: Specifications
text: [x] | mgcd | cells, grid, q | MGCD cell centroids, great circle distance quantile. | [x] | mgcd | coords, q | maximum great circle distance points | [x] | cenrad | coords, q | Centroid radius points, maximum quantile distance. | [x] | cenrad | cells, q | Centroid radius points, maximum quantile distance. | [x] | latrange | coords | latitudinal range point cloud. | [ ] | zoneaarea | coords | spherical surface area zone defined latitudinal range point cloud. | [ ] | zoneaarea | coords/cells, grid | latitudinal range point cloud. | [x] | mstlength | coords | length minimum spanning tree based set points | [ ] | mstlength | cells, grid | length minimum spanning tree based centers cells. | [ ] | chull_cell | coords, grid | number cells convex hull occupies. | [ ] | chull_cell | cells, grid | number cells convex hull occupies. | [ ] | ahull_cell | cells, grid | number cells covered alpha hull surface sphere. | [ ] | ahull_cell | coords, grid | number cells covered alpha hull surface sphere. | [ ] | mincircle | coords, q | minimum small circle area, radius position covers q proportion points | [ ] | minellipse | coords, q | covering spherical ellipse set points, shorter longer axis . | [ ] | density | coords, q | densitiy occurrence records |

what: Spherical Ranges and Parametrization of Geographic Shapes
title: Spherical Ranges and Parametrization of Geographic Shapes
text: package collation tools metrics characterize distribution data surfrace sphere ellipsoid. primary group metrics describe extent distribution, .e. geographic ranges. calculation geographic ranges can executed using point coordinates data, vector polygons, well cells discretized sphere. Besides ensuring use geometrically correct implementations, package offers exploration partial results visual diagnostics.

title: Spherical Ranges and Parametrization of Geographic Shapes
text: functions package dependent geographic discretization structures, dependencies package - primaril vector-spatial objects icosahedral grids, implemented sf icosa packages, respectively.

title: Spherical Ranges and Parametrization of Geographic Shapes
code: library(orange)  # background map library(chronosphere) ne <- fetch("NaturalEarth", verbose=FALSE)  # some example raw data (Pinna nobilis from OBIS) data(pinna) # only coords coords <- unique(pinna[, c("decimallongitude", "decimallatitude")])  # rename to standard colnames(coords) <- c("long", "lat")  # omit anomaly  coords <- coords[-855, ]   # basic plot plot(ne$geometry, reset=FALSE, xlim=c(-15, 40), ylim=c(30, 50),     col="#ffe7ba", border=NA) points(coords, pch=3, col="#5d7327", cex=2)  # Maximum great circle distance mgcd <- mgcd(coords, plot=TRUE)  # Occupancy on a hexagonal grid hex <- hexagrid(deg=2, sf=TRUE) # from package icosa occ <- occupancy(coords, icosa=hex, plot=TRUE)  # Minimum spanning tree length mst <- mstlength(coords, plot=TRUE, plot.args=list(lwd=3)) # Maximum great circle distance mgcd ## $estimate ## [1] 3452.84 ##  ## $index ##      row col ## [1,] 786 851 # Occupancy count  occ ## [1] 23

title: Calculate range with the centroid-radius method — cenrad
code: # 1. Canvas hex <- hexagrid(deg=5, sf=TRUE) #> Selecting hexagrid with tessellation vector: c(2, 4). #> Mean edge length: 4.993 degrees. plot(hex, reset=FALSE, xlim=c(-15, 40), ylim=c(25, 63))  # 2. Records data(pinna)  # just the coordinates coordMat <- SimpleCoordinates(pinna, long="decimallongitude", lat="decimallatitude") points(coordMat)  # 3. calculate and visualize centDist <- cenrad(coordMat, plot=TRUE)  lines(rbind(centDist$centroid,     coordMat[which(centDist$estimate==centDist$distances)[1],]),     col="blue", lwd=3)

text: list two elements, center o small circle surface radius small circle.

title: Identify the center of a small circle based on three points of a sphere — circumcenter
code: # generate 3 points on a sphere ps <- icosa::rpsphere(3, output="polar") small <- circumcenter(x=ps) circle<- smallcircles(x=small$center, r=small$r, output="polar")  plot(NULL, NULL, xlim=c(-180, 180), ylim=c(-90, 90)) points(ps, col="red", pch=3, cex=4) points(small$center, col="blue", pch=16) points(circle, col="green", pch=16)

title: Samples generated from various Kent distributions — kentsamples
text: liest 16 matrices (1000 x 2) columns equal longitudes latitudes. scientificname Taxon name. central_s Central position, small spread. central_m Central position, medium spread. central_l Central position, large spread. central_xl Central position, extra large spread. arctic_s Arctic position, small spread. arctic_m Arctic position, medium spread. arctic_l Arctic position, large spread. arctic_xl Arctic position, extra large spread. antarctic_s Antarctic position, small spread. antarctic_m Antarctic position, medium spread. antarctic_l Antarctic position, large spread. antarctic_xl Antarctic position, extra large spread. dateline_s Dateline position, small spread. dateline_m Dateline position, medium spread. dateline_l Dateline position, large spread. dateline_xl Dateline position, extra large spread.

title: Samples generated from various Kent distributions — kentsamples
text: set coordinates generated Kent distributions sample number n = 1000. samples elements list, 16 total combination location spread (.e. kappa parameter Kent distribution). four locations: central (0°N 0°E), arctic (85°N 5°E), antarctic (85°S 5°E) dateline (170°E 5°N), four sizes: small (s, kappa = 50), medium (m, kappa = 15), large (l, kappa = 5), extra-large (xl, kappa = 2).
